bead.title: Make HELIX routing skill runtime-neutral
bead.description:
Context: The collapsed HELIX scope says HELIX is methodology, artifact catalog, and one runtime-neutral routing skill. It explicitly does not provide a CLI, tracker, queue, execution loop, or runtime infrastructure. Evidence: `docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md` lines 16-19, 90, 157-167, and `docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md` lines 167-179 and 201-208. Current `skills/helix/SKILL.md` still routes beads, queue, build/run, commit, worker, and tracker concerns, including hits such as `beads/work items`, `queue`, `HELIX/DDx`, `work tracker`, and `background HELIX operator loop`.

In-scope files:
- skills/helix/SKILL.md
- docs/install/* only if a runtime-specific note must move out of the skill body
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-adapter-boundary.md only for directly related references

Out-of-scope:
- Do not reintroduce public `helix-*` skills.
- Do not edit DDx runtime skill files under `.agents/skills/ddx` or `.claude/skills/ddx`.
- Do not remove methodology routes that remain runtime-neutral.

Implementation notes: keep the public skill as the single HELIX entrypoint, but make execution/tracker concepts runtime-neutral or move DDx-specific guidance to install/adapter docs. Preserve the align taxonomy and gap handoff contract.
bead.acceptance:
Command: bash tests/validate-skills.sh
Expected: exits 0.

Command: bash -lc '! rg -n "DDx|ddx |execute-bead|\\[ddx-|\\.ddx/|\\bbeads?\\b|\\bqueue\\b|\\btracker\\b|background HELIX run|operator loop" skills/helix/SKILL.md'
Expected: exits 0.

Command: git diff --check
Expected: exits 0.
